title: Keyboard Translation Table Syntax
text: Keyboard Translation Table Syntax Keyboard translation tables map input codes from keyboards or database to logical Uniface functions and characters. {^^ CommentText } meta_ Variant ^keyboard | ^dbms { Description } CharacterMappings Copy Each character mapping has the following syntax: CodeSequence ^ Mnemonic | { FontNumber . } Character { Description } Arguments ^^ CommentText —two carets ( ^^ ) anywhere on a line indicate a comment line. Meta_Variant —identifies the internal character set that is used to translate between Unicode and a supported external character set. For example, meta_western or Unicode . For more information, see Meta_Variant and Meta Variants . CharacterMappings —one or more lines containing up to 1024 characters mapping a sequence of keyboard codes to a Uniface function or meta character. CodeSequence —character sequences sent by the keyboard. Consult the keyboard documentation for details. Mnemonic —Uniface function represented by a supported Uniface mnemonic or internal code. They can be entered in uppercase or lowercase. It is recommended that you always precede a Uniface function with a caret ( ^ ), since it is mandatory when mapping several Uniface functions to a single set of keyboard codes and it improves the clarity of the definitions. FontNumber —optional; if omitted, the default is Font 0. Character —literal character, its decimal value, or a Unicode ID. For example, the positions 1.A and 1.^065 are equivalent. Description —optional comments. Description Use keyboard translation tables to define mappings for: Structure Editor Function Mappings Default Key Combinations Macro Definitions User Key Definitions Uniface reads the definitions in a keyboard translation table sequentially from top to bottom. If any key is defined more than once, the last (lowest in table) is used. If your definitions are being ignored, check to make sure that your settings are not being overridden later on in the table. Example: MswinX_Latin1 The MswwinX_Latin1 keyboard translation table contains the following content. Ellipsis (...) indicated information that has been left out for the sake of brevity. You can examine the complete table in the Uniface IDE. Meta_western ^keyboard ^^ Keyboard translation table: MSWINX ^^ Characterset ^^ Windows ANSI_CHARSET, 8-bit ^^ Prefixes ^^ None ANSI Character ^^ ^128 Function ^^ ^129 Function, Shift ^^ ... snipped here ^^ Base definitions: Gold is Gray + ^136+^SP ^SUPER ^136+ ^GOLD ^CHR GOLD_BASE_SET ^^ Printable characters ^CHR FONT_0 32-126 ASCII/ANSI 7 bit ^CHR/8 FONT_1 160-254 ANSI 8 bit ^^ almost common in mswin3/mswinx to map the 0x80 - 0xA0 control range ^144^128 ^u20AC ^^EURO SIGN ^144^129 ^u0403 ^^CYRILLIC CAPITAL LETTER GJE ^144^130 ^u201A ^^SINGLE LOW-9 QUOTATION MARK ^144^131 ^u0192 ^^LATIN SMALL LETTER F WITH HOOK ^144^132 ^u201E ^^DOUBLE LOW-9 QUOTATION MARK ^144^133 ^u2026 ^^HORIZONTAL ELLIPSIS ^144^134 ^u2020 ^^DAGGER ^144^135 ^u2021 ^^DOUBLE DAGGER ^^ ... snipped here ^^ Left+Right click is Detail ^128^163 ^DETAIL ^^ Other mouse buttons unassigned ^128^164 ^NUL M click ^128^173 ^NUL L+R dblclk ^128^174 ^NUL M dblclk ^129^163 ^NUL L+R shift-click ^129^164 ^NUL M shift-click ^^ END Copy Related Links Keyboard Translation Tables A keyboard translation table maps a sequence of input codes received from a keyboard or database to logical function codes and characters recognized by Uniface.
